com.pax.poslink.peripheries

Class POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.pax.poslink.peripheries.POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter
  • Enclosing class:
    POSLinkPrinter

    public static class POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter
extends java.lang.Object
    Used to format the string for POSLinkPrinter.print(String, PrintListener, boolean...)

    An Example:

    printDataFormatter .addHeader() .addLineSeparator() .addLeftAlign().addContent("left").addCenterAlign().addContent("center").addRightAlign().addNormalFont().addContent("right") .addLineSeparator() .addDate() .addLineSeparator() .addCenterAlign().addTime() .addLineSeparator() .addBigFont().addSN() .addLineSeparator() .addDisclaimer() .addTrailer();

    Not all controller content in API Guide are supported.
    \$eSig, \$BARD \$Logo are not supported.

      PrintDataFormatter(java.lang.String src)
      New PrintDataFormatter with original string.

    • Constructor Detail

      • PrintDataFormatter

        public PrintDataFormatter()

      • PrintDataFormatter

        public PrintDataFormatter(java.lang.String src)
        New PrintDataFormatter with original string.
        Parameters:
        src - The source string.

    • Method Detail

      • set

        public void set(java.lang.String src)
        Replace all data with src
        Parameters:
        src - The input print data.

      • addLeftAlign

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addLeftAlign()
        Use to indicate this is the left string. It will append \L

      • addCenterAlign

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addCenterAlign()
        Use to indicate this is the center string. It will append \C

      • addRightAlign

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addRightAlign()
        Use to indicate this is the right string. It will append \R

      • addContent

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addContent(java.lang.String content)
        Add pure string content
        Parameters:
        content - content

      • addLineSeparator

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addLineSeparator()
        Line Separator. It will append \n

      • addDate

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addDate()
        Current date in MM/dd/yyyy.format. It will append \$Date

      • addTime

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addTime()
        Current time in hh:mm:ss format. It will append \$Time
        Returns:
        this

      • addSmallFont

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addSmallFont()
        Make content printed in small font. It will append \1
        Returns:
        this

      • addNormalFont

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addNormalFont()
        Make content printed in normal font. It will append \2
        Returns:
        this

      • addBigFont

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addBigFont()
        Make content printed in big font. It will append \3
        Returns:
        this

      • addHeader

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addHeader()
        Print header information. Usually it is a separate line. It will append \$Header It starts and terminates at new line, center align.
        Returns:
        this

      • addTrailer

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addTrailer()
        Print trailer information. Usually it is a separate line. It will append \$Trailer It starts and terminates at new line, center align.
        Returns:
        this

      • addSN

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addSN()
        Print terminal SN. It will append \$SN#.
        Returns:
        this

      • addInvert

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addInvert()
        Used to invert color for content. It will append \I
        Returns:
        this

      • addDisclaimer

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addDisclaimer()
        Print standard disclaimer information. Usually it is a separate line. It will append \$Disclaimer It starts and terminates at new line, center align.
        Returns:
        this

      • addBarcode

        public POSLinkPrinter.PrintDataFormatter addBarcode(int type,
                                                    int blockSize,
                                                    java.lang.String content)
        Convert barcode content to barcode.(Requires zxing library)
        Parameters:
        type - see PrintBarcode.BarCodeType
        blockSize - A minimum block that can be recognized, it is made up by square which has number of size level printing points in every row and column. Value can only be: 1,2, 4, 8, 12, or 16, and other values are invalid.
        content - string content of code
        Returns:
        this

      • clear

        public void clear()
        Clear all string you add.

      • build

        public java.lang.String build()
        Returns:
        The formatted string to print. POSLinkPrinter.print(String, PrintListener, boolean...)
